title: Credential Compromise: Card Used Across Unprecedented Product Category Mix
narrative: A compromised card is being weaponized across multiple ProductCD types (W, C, H, S, R) that the legitimate cardholder never accessed together. The fraudster tests the credential in diverse merchant categories to maximize monetization before the card is blocked. Individual transactions appear unremarkable—amounts fit spending patterns, velocity is controlled, and billing data validates. However, card-level counters reveal the card is suddenly transacting across 3-5 distinct product categories within a rolling window, far exceeding the cardholder's historical product diversity. The C14 counter (distinct product types) spikes while C8 (card-merchant pairs) shows the card hitting new merchants in each category. Device fingerprints show first-time pairings with the card across these product surfaces. This cross-category exploitation pattern is invisible at the transaction level but unmistakable in aggregate card behavior.
indicators: C14 >= 3 indicating card accessed three or more distinct ProductCD types in rolling window
card1_distinct_products > 2.5 (p75+ baseline), elevated unique product category count for this card
C8 > 6 (p75 threshold), card appearing at multiple new merchant identifiers
D1 = 0 on majority of recent transactions, indicating first-time device-card pairings across product types
C1 > 12 (p75), elevated card transaction counter despite controlled per-transaction timing
card1_amt_zscore between -1.0 and +1.0, amounts remain within cardholder's normal spending range
indicator_explanations: The card has been used across three or more different product categories recently
This card's product category diversity far exceeds its historical shopping pattern
The card is appearing at many merchants it has never visited before
Multiple devices are being paired with this card for the first time simultaneously
Card-level activity shows elevated usage despite each transaction appearing individually normal
Transaction amounts fit the cardholder's typical spending range, masking the fraud activity
summary: A stolen card is exploited across multiple product categories (W, C, H, S, R) that the cardholder never historically accessed together, with C14 and card1_distinct_products counters revealing unprecedented product diversity while individual transaction behavior remains unremarkable.
reasoning: Legitimate cardholders typically shop within 1-2 consistent product categories. When a card suddenly transacts across 3+ distinct ProductCD types with new devices and merchants but controlled amounts, it indicates a fraudster systematically testing the credential across multiple monetization surfaces before the account is locked.
